questions.question: How do you create a new button in a Notion page?

options.text: Type '+' in the page and select Button
options.explanation: You must use the slash command to find the Button option.

options.text: Type '/' in the page, then find and select Button
options.explanation: This is the standard way to create a button; the slash command opens the menu to find the Button block.

options.text: Drag the Button icon from the sidebar into the page
options.explanation: Buttons are not draggable from the sidebar; they must be created inline using the slash command.

options.text: Go to Settings and select 'Create Button'
options.explanation: Buttons are created per page, not in global settings.

questions.question: What permissions are required to create, edit, and click a button on a page?

options.explanation: Viewers cannot create or interact with buttons.

options.text: Full access or Can edit permissions
options.explanation: Users with Full access or Can edit permissions can create and click buttons on a page.
Comment permissions do not allow for button creation or interaction.
You must have specific editing permissions to interact with a button.

questions.question: Which of the following button actions is only available on paid plans?

options.explanation: Inserting blocks is available on all plans.
Sending notifications is available on all plans.
The 'Send mail to' action requires a paid plan.
Defining variables is available on all plans.

questions.question: Can formulas be used in the 'Insert blocks' button action?

options.explanation: Formulas have specific limitations and cannot be used in Insert blocks.

options.text: Yes, but only if you use the @ symbol
options.explanation: Formulas cannot be used in this specific action regardless of symbols.

options.text: No, formulas cannot be used in Insert blocks
options.explanation: The article explicitly states formulas cannot be used in Inserting blocks.

options.text: Yes, if the blocks are toggles
options.explanation: The restriction applies to the action type, not the specific block type.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of people you can send a notification to in a single 'Send notification to' action?

options.explanation: The limit is higher than 10.
You can send a notification to up to 20 people in your workspace or associated with a People property.
The limit is 20, not 50.
There is a hard limit of 20 recipients for this action.

questions.question: If a button automation encounters an error while you are setting it up, what happens?

options.text: It saves the automation and fixes it later
options.explanation: You cannot save the automation until you correct the errors.

options.text: You cannot save the automation until you make the required corrections
options.explanation: Notion prevents saving until all setup errors are resolved.

options.text: It automatically redirects you to the help center
options.explanation: The error is displayed in the UI, but it doesn't redirect you away from the editor.

options.text: The button works normally in the background
options.explanation: The automation is invalid and will not function until errors are fixed.

questions.question: Which button action adds a page to a database and allows you to edit the properties of that new page?

options.explanation: This action edits existing pages, not creates a new one.
This specific action creates a new page in the selected database and lets you configure its properties.
This action simply opens an existing page or link, it does not create a new page.
This action creates variables for use in other actions, it doesn't manage database pages.

questions.question: Which plans are required to use the 'Send Slack notification to' action?

options.explanation: This is a premium feature restricted to specific paid tiers.
This feature is exclusive to Plus, Business, and Enterprise plans.
Personal Pro plans do not have access to this action.
This action is not available on the free or Personal Pro tiers.

questions.question: Why might a button fail to name a created page properly if a template is used?

options.text: The user lacks permission to edit the database
options.explanation: While permissions are needed for editing, the naming issue is specific to template logic.

options.text: The template values overwrite the values from the button
options.explanation: When using a template within a button, the data defined in the template takes precedence over the button's default naming.

options.text: The button action 'Open page or URL' was selected instead
options.explanation: The naming issue relates to the template logic, not the action type used to open it.

options.text: The page is set to 'Private' status
options.explanation: Private pages can be created by buttons; this is not the cause of the naming issue.

questions.question: Which action sends an HTTP POST request to a specific URL?

options.explanation: Send mail to sends emails via Gmail, not HTTP requests.
The 'Send webhook' action is designed to send HTTP POST requests to external services.
This action is for creating variables, not communicating with external APIs.
This sends a custom notification to a Slack channel, not a generic HTTP request.
